name: Hoyo de Manzanares and Colmenar Viejo Loop
name_original: Hoyo de Manzanares, Colmenar Viejo, Cerceda – ruta circular
description: This moderate 34.2-mile road cycling route near Madrid offers scenic views of granite formations and forests, passing through charming towns
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hoyo De Manzanares traverse a varied terrain within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, approximately 35 kilometers from Madrid. The landscape features rolling hills, extensive holm oak and juniper forests, and distinctive granite rock formations. Rivers like the Manzanares and Guadarrama, alongside reservoirs such as Valmayor, define parts of the area, offering diverse scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Hoyo De Manzanares The most popular road cycling route is Hoyo de Manzanares and Colmenar Viejo Loop, a 34.2 miles (55.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route explores the local towns and includes points of interest like the Grajal Bridge over the Manzanares river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Guadarrama Towns Loop, a moderate 36.3 miles (58.4 km) path. This route offers views of the diverse landscapes influenced by the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Local road cyclists also love the San Fernando Bridge – Manzanares River loop from Torrelodones, a 60.9 miles (97.9 km) trail leading through areas defined by the Manzanares River, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Hoyo De Manzanares is defined by rolling hills, extensive holm oak and juniper forests, and characteristic granite rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Hoyo De Manzanares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 14,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoyo De Manzanares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 130 tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate paths to more challenging rides.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: The road cycling routes in Hoyo de Manzanares offer a diverse range of difficulties. You'll find 17 easy routes, 69 moderate routes, and 47 difficult routes, ensuring options for cyclists of all experience levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features will I encounter on the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Hoyo de Manzanares traverse a rich natural environment. You can expect rolling hills, extensive holm oak and juniper forests, and distinctive granite rock formations. The area is part of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, offering views of the Sierra de Hoyo and proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Rivers like the Manzanares and Guadarrama, alongside reservoirs such as Valmayor, also define parts of the scenery.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic viewpoints and pass by notable landmarks. For instance, the Monte Ejido features the Mirador de Monte Egido with excellent views. You can also explore routes that pass by significant bodies of water like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Historic sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible on some tours, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle – Santillana Reservoir loop from Torrelodones&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: The road cycling experience in Hoyo de Manzanares is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, the variety of trails available, and the immersive experience within the protected natural parks.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18600131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Fernando Bridge – Manzanares River loop from Torrelodones&lt;/a&gt; feature over 940 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31804618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Valmayor Reservoir – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Galapagar - La Navata&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 920 meters of ascent.

Q: Is Hoyo de Manzanares accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares is located approximately 35 kilometers from Madrid, making it reachable by public transport. While specific bicycle transport policies vary, regional bus services often connect the area to larger towns, which may offer options for cyclists. It's advisable to check with local transport providers for current regulations regarding bikes.

Q: Do I need any special permits to cycle in the protected natural areas around Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares is situated within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and near the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. While general road cycling on public roads typically doesn't require permits, it's always recommended to respect local regulations, stay on marked routes, and be aware of any specific restrictions that might apply to certain protected zones or trails within the parks. For detailed information, consult the official park authorities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially midday, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, though the extensive holm oak and juniper forests can provide some shelter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Hoyo de Manzanares are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977460304/sierra-de-guadarrama-towns-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama Towns Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a comprehensive tour of the area's diverse landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking when planning a road cycling trip in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town centers of Hoyo de Manzanares and other nearby municipalities that serve as starting points for routes. Look for designated parking areas in towns or near popular trailheads. As the area is popular for outdoor activities, some routes may have specific parking facilities indicated.
tips: The town is very charming with its stone houses and is full on weekends with people hiking, climbing and biking. In the square there is an Information Office where you can get a wide variety of information.
Factory bridge over the Manzanares on the M-618 between Colmenar Viejo and Hoyo del Manzanares. Wonderful views
Probably its star monument due to its strange and unusual existence is the Entreterminos dolmen, an authentic megalith dating from around the 4th millennium BC. c.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered a moderate road ride, suitable for cyclists with good fitness. The terrain features rolling hills, extensive pine and holm oak forests, and distinctive granite rock formations. You'll primarily encounter well-paved surfaces, making it enjoyable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse natural features within the protected Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, influenced by the nearby Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You can expect views of natural parks, granite outcrops, and the Valmayor Reservoir. The route also passes through charming towns like Hoyo de Manzanares, known for its stone houses.

faqs.question: What are the notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include passing through the picturesque town of Hoyo de Manzanares, known for its stone houses, and crossing the historic Grajal Bridge. You'll also ride near the Collado Villalba Town Center.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Hoyo de Manzanares, Colmenar Viejo, or Cerceda. Look for public parking lots or street parking in these municipalities.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to the towns along the route, like Hoyo de Manzanares, Colmenar Viejo, and Cerceda, are available from Madrid. Buses serve these areas, but check local timetables for specific routes and schedules to plan your start point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder. Summers can be hot in the Madrid region, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, it is generally not recommended for dogs due to safety concerns with traffic. If you wish to explore the area with a dog, consider nearby hiking trails within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, where leashed dogs are often permitted.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of several official trails, including Ruta ADESGAM, GR 124 MTB (Senda Real), Senda Real, Madrid Way of Saint James, and Cañada Real Segoviana, among others. However, the primary focus of this route is road cycling.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Hoyo de Manzanares, Colmenar Viejo, and Cerceda, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and distance of approximately 55 km, this route is best suited for cyclists with good fitness. It may not be ideal for young children or families looking for a very easy, leisurely ride. More experienced cycling families might enjoy it.
